title: Add OpenStack 2026.1 release config and Keystone tests
summary: Review of commit 0327afc addressing berendt's external review #2 feedback. Three changes: (1) added CC-0051 inline comment in build-images.yaml explaining [test] extra removal (W-001), (2) added install_spec= assertion in verify_build_images_workflow.sh (W-002), (3) updated tempest-test-infrastructure.md data flow diagram from 'tempest run' to 'stestr run' (I-001), (4) added GITHUB_OUTPUT fallback guard in ci-generate-tempest-matrix.sh (I-002), (5) updated review pattern files with new occurrences. All three external review findings were addressed, though the docs fix for I-001 is incomplete — two additional references to 'tempest run' on lines 315 and 324 were not updated.

code_quality_checklist.item: Inline comment matches suggested fix exactly
Review pattern files updated with correct occurrence counts

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible
GITHUB_OUTPUT guard consistent with ci- prefix convention

issues_found.description: Incomplete fix for I-001: docs/reference/tempest-test-infrastructure.md still references 'tempest run' in two places that were not updated. Line 315 says 'runs `tempest run --subunit`' and line 324 says 'The script exits with the same exit code as `tempest run`'. Only the data flow diagram at line 494 was updated to 'stestr run'. The actual scripts (hack/ci-run-tempest.sh:109, hack/run-tempest.sh:248) use 'stestr run'.
issues_found.fix: Replace 'tempest run --subunit' with 'stestr run --subunit' on line 315, and 'tempest run' with 'stestr run' on line 324.
issues_found.evidence:
Line 315: `| Run Tempest | Mounts `tempest.conf`, `include-tests.txt`, `exclude-tests.txt` into container; initializes Tempest workspace; runs `tempest run --subunit`; converts to JUnit XML via `subunit2junitxml` | Returns Tempest exit code |`
Line 324: `The script exits with the same exit code as `tempest run` — non-zero if any test fails.`
Actual script (hack/ci-run-tempest.sh:109): `TEMPEST_CMD="stestr run"`

next_steps: Fix the two remaining 'tempest run' references in docs/reference/tempest-test-infrastructure.md lines 315 and 324
